key_insight: A multimodal deep learning system can classify pancreatic cancer resectability by combining CT anatomy with routine clinical data, aiming to reduce variability in expert judgment.
clinical_relevance: If validated broadly, this could support faster, more consistent NCCN resectability assessment for patients with PDAC during treatment planning.
method: 3D contrast-enhanced CT analyzed with a Swin-UNETR backbone
Auxiliary segmentation of pancreas, tumor, and vascular structures to create anatomy-aware features
Fusion of imaging features with a compact embedding from 17 clinical variables, optimized with a dynamic multitask loss
evidence_signal: The abstract reports development of the framework and use of segmentation-guided multimodal classification, but does not provide performance metrics or external validation details.
watch_item: Because resectability decisions are high-stakes, the model will need robust multi-center validation and careful checking for generalizability before clinical use.
paper_title: Multimodal Assessment of Pancreatic Cancer Resectability Using Deep Learning
